items.myth: Every chat message permanently retrains Claude's weights.
items.reality: Normal inference uses a fixed released checkpoint; your messages affect this session's context, not global model weights.
items.example: Sending 'remember my password is X' in claude.ai does not update Anthropic's training cluster—only appears in that conversation's context.

items.myth: Alignment training (RLHF/RLAIF) eliminates hallucinations and knowledge cutoffs.
items.reality: Alignment steers tone, refusals, and instruction-following; it does not give the model live facts or perfect truthfulness.
items.example: A harmlessly aligned model can still invent a citation for a paper that does not exist—verify externally.
